hypothesis: If models are trained semi-supervised by minimizing each ensemble member’s divergence to a stop‑gradient ensemble mean on unlabeled data in addition to supervised loss, then with limited labels they will achieve lower error and better calibration than supervised, mean‑teacher, and pseudo‑labeling baselines across molecular graph and image benchmarks—improving further with larger ensemble size—because consensus targets provide stable, low‑variance pseudo‑labels that mitigate confirmation bias.
